skill.name: industrial-brutalist-ui
skill.description: Raw mechanical interfaces fusing Swiss typographic print with military terminal aesthetics. Rigid grids, extreme type scale contrast, utilitarian color, analog degradation effects. For data-heavy dashboards, portfolios, or editorial sites that need to feel like declassified blueprints.
skill.summary: Create industrial brutalist web interfaces with Swiss typography and military terminal aesthetics
skill.author: leonxlnx

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 71 potential security issues, all are FALSE POSITIVES. The backtick patterns are CSS code examples in markdown documentation, not shell execution. Color hex codes were misidentified as cryptographic weaknesses. ASCII bracket decorations were misidentified as Windows system references. This skill contains only design guidelines with no executable code.

content.value_statement: Create bold, mechanical web interfaces with Swiss typography and military terminal aesthetics. Generate data-heavy dashboards and editorial sites with raw functionality and extreme typographic contrast.
actual_capabilities: Generate CSS for Swiss industrial print-style interfaces with rigid grids and heavy typography
Create tactical telemetry interfaces with dark mode CRT effects and monospaced typography
Design layouts with extreme typographic scale contrast using clamp() fluid typography
Implement analog degradation effects including halftones, scanlines, and dithering
Build mathematically engineered grid systems with visible compartmentalization
Apply utilitarian color palettes for light and dark substrate modes
limitations: This skill provides design guidance and CSS code only, not functional application logic
Requires manual implementation of responsive breakpoints beyond basic clamp() functions
Does not generate interactive JavaScript behaviors or state management
Typography recommendations depend on external web fonts that must be sourced separately

use_cases.title: Data Dashboard Design
use_cases.description: Create high-density monitoring dashboards for analytics platforms with tactical terminal aesthetics, monospaced telemetry data, and clear visual hierarchy
use_cases.target_user: Frontend developers building analytics or monitoring interfaces

use_cases.title: Editorial Portfolio Site
use_cases.description: Build visually striking portfolio or editorial websites using Swiss industrial print aesthetics with oversized typography and asymmetric layouts

use_cases.title: Technical Documentation UI
use_cases.description: Design documentation interfaces that feel like declassified blueprints or military manuals with rigid grids and utilitarian styling

prompt_templates.title: Basic Brutalist Landing Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a landing page using Swiss industrial print style. Use a light substrate with #F4F4F0 background, heavy sans-serif headers at massive scale, and red #E61919 accents. Include visible grid lines and uppercase monospace metadata.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new project with light-mode industrial aesthetic

prompt_templates.title: Dark Terminal Dashboard
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a data dashboard using tactical telemetry aesthetics. Use dark mode with #0A0A0A background, JetBrains Mono font for all data displays, white phosphor #EAEAEA text, and optional green #4AF626 for one status indicator only.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating a high-density data visualization interface

prompt_templates.title: Typography System Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate CSS typography variables for a brutalist interface with macro-headers using Inter Black at clamp(4rem, 10vw, 15rem) with -0.05em letter-spacing, and micro-text using JetBrains Mono at 12px with 0.08em tracking. All uppercase.
prompt_templates.scenario: Establishing the typographic scale and spacing system

prompt_templates.title: Analog Texture Effects
prompt_templates.prompt: Add CSS effects for analog degradation including halftone pattern overlay using SVG radial dots, CRT scanlines with repeating-linear-gradient, and global mechanical noise using SVG filter on the root element.
prompt_templates.scenario: Applying post-processing effects to reduce digital perfection

output_examples.input: Create a hero section with industrial brutalist style
output_examples.output: Generates CSS Grid layout with massive uppercase headline spanning full viewport width, visible 1px borders dividing sections, monospace metadata labels in corners, and red accent lines marking key data points.

output_examples.input: Design a data table for tactical terminal interface
output_examples.output: Creates table with fixed-width monospace font, dark background, white text, optional green status cell, horizontal rules spanning full width, and bracket-framed section headers like [ SYSTEM STATUS ].

best_practices: Choose ONE visual archetype (Swiss Industrial Print or Tactical Telemetry) per project and never mix both in the same interface
Always use CSS clamp() functions for macro-typography to ensure fluid scaling across viewport sizes while maintaining impact
Apply global noise filters and texture effects at the DOM root level for consistent analog degradation across all elements
anti_patterns: Never mix light and dark substrate color palettes within the same interface - commit to one mode
Avoid using border-radius on any elements - all corners must be exactly 90 degrees for mechanical rigidity
Do not use gradients, soft drop shadows, or modern translucency effects as they violate the utilitarian aesthetic

faq.question: Can I mix Swiss Industrial Print and Tactical Telemetry styles in one project?
faq.answer: No. The skill requires choosing ONE visual archetype per project. Mixing light and dark substrates, or combining the two distinct typographic systems, breaks the aesthetic consistency.

faq.question: What web fonts work best for this style?
faq.answer: For macro-headers use Neue Haas Grotesk Black, Inter Extra Bold, Archivo Black, or Monument Extended. For data text use JetBrains Mono, IBM Plex Mono, or Space Mono. All fonts should be loaded from web font services.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for e-commerce or consumer applications?
faq.answer: This aesthetic is intentionally raw and mechanical. It works best for data dashboards, portfolios, editorial sites, and technical documentation. For consumer-focused e-commerce, consider softer UI patterns.

faq.question: How do I implement the halftone and dithering effects?
faq.answer: Use SVG radial dot patterns with mix-blend-mode: multiply overlay, or apply pre-processed dithered images. The skill provides CSS patterns for creating dot-matrix texturing on any element.

faq.question: Can I use color beyond the specified red accent?
faq.answer: No. The color system is strictly limited to the background/foreground palette plus aviation red #E61919 or #FF2A2A. The only exception is terminal green #4AF626 for a single specific UI element when needed.

faq.question: Does this skill work with React, Vue, or other frameworks?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill generates CSS and design patterns that work with any frontend framework. Apply the CSS to your components whether using plain HTML, React styled-components, Vue scoped styles, or Tailwind custom classes.
